jokes.text: Can you spare a little change for an old buccaneer?
jokes.setup: Larry in pirate costume continuing the bit
jokes.punchline: Asking for change like a homeless person but as a buccaneer
jokes.explanation: Good character work, Larry's commitment to absurd scenarios

jokes.text: Don't answer it. I don't want to talk to her... I'm not here.
jokes.setup: Phone rings after they watch a show
jokes.punchline: Larry desperately avoiding a phone call from someone who always calls
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry avoidance behavior, relatable social awkwardness

jokes.text: I can't believe this woman has to call after every single show... Every... I mean, does it ever end with this lady?
jokes.setup: Woman calls with over-the-top praise after watching show
jokes.punchline: Larry's exasperation at predictable fan behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about celebrity interactions

jokes.text: You would entrust me that? A gift for a baby?
jokes.setup: Cheryl asks Larry to buy a baby shower gift
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous reaction to being trusted with such responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-awareness about his social incompetence

jokes.text: Get a doll for Betty. It's her baby shower. Well, what about the surrogate? Get her anything?
jokes.setup: Instructions to buy gift for Betty's baby shower
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately identifies social awkwardness of surrogate situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry finding social complications others miss

jokes.text: The surrogate's standing there like an idiot and has nothing to open? What's the surrogate etiquette?
jokes.setup: Larry worrying about surrogate at baby shower
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex emotional situation to etiquette question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - reducing emotional complexity to social rules

jokes.text: Because the dog bit your penis? Yes.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining why he can't do something
jokes.punchline: Casually confirming dog bit his penis
jokes.explanation: Absurd situation delivered with perfect economy and matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: That magazine collection, you really need to do something about that... just making an observation.
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive complaint disguised as helpful observation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Larry's magazine rant, realistic workplace passive-aggression

jokes.text: What are you doing, stealing your magazines from garbage cans? I have never seen such a collection of shit in my life.
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-the-top accusation about garbage can theft
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry escalation from minor annoyance to extreme accusation

jokes.text: 'People' magazine. 'Tom Cruise is 40!' 'Emilio Estevez is 40!' 'Good for them, they're 40!'
jokes.punchline: Mock celebration of celebrities reaching mundane milestone
jokes.explanation: Great observational comedy about celebrity culture and aging

jokes.text: It sounds like you're reading a lot of these magazines you don't like... you got a lot of that stuff memorized.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about magazines in detail
jokes.punchline: Nurse pointing out he's clearly reading them thoroughly
jokes.explanation: Good comeback that exposes Larry's hypocrisy

jokes.text: We were going along great, and you kind of spiked a little there.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining test results to Larry
jokes.punchline: Casual understatement of potential heart problem
jokes.explanation: Medical understatement creates tension and humor

jokes.text: I'm gonna give you a device called a heart halter.
jokes.punchline: Heart halter sounds like horse equipment
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay setup for visual gag, but not strongly crafted

jokes.text: Have you ever made it with an African-American? Yes. Twice. What, the same one twice? Two different ones. Same time? No, same time... two different ones.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking about Larry's sexual experience
jokes.punchline: Larry's confusion about 'same time' meaning
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection joke but relies on sexual content

jokes.text: Big? They could put the Chicago fire out with this. It was... it was a joke. It was like from another planet.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd comparisons
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but relies on crude humor

jokes.text: Larry's practical suggestion: 'You need to see a guard. Did you see a guard? You can't tell by a forward. They're too big, out of proportion.'
jokes.punchline: Larry applying statistical analysis to intimate concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - applying rational analysis to irrational fears with basketball expertise

jokes.text: I could see how it could be a concern... maybe I can step in... if you're too intimidated to go through with it, I could try.
jokes.punchline: Larry offering to have sex with Jeff's date
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's inappropriate problem-solving

jokes.text: A good friend doesn't do that... I'd rather go to Tiffany's and get you a bowl.
jokes.punchline: Jeff preferring expensive gift to sexual favor
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response with unexpected comparison

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm not the valet. Oh, I'm sor... It is possible for black people to have other jobs.
jokes.setup: Larry hands keys to black man
jokes.punchline: Man calling out racist assumption
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb cringe moment exposing unconscious bias

jokes.text: So you automatically think the black man is the valet, huh?
jokes.punchline: Direct confrontation about racial profiling
jokes.explanation: Escalates the cringe with character who won't let it go

jokes.text: You would have asked him for a stock tip or... where do you golf?
jokes.punchline: Contrasting assumptions made about white men in suits
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered as comedy, exposes racial double standard perfectly

jokes.text: You've slept with a lot of white men and black men, I'm sure. Is there any discernible difference?
jokes.punchline: Assuming Wanda has extensive sexual experience
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate question, good cringe factor

jokes.text: Why would you ask me something like that, Larry? My friend wants to know.
jokes.punchline: Larry blaming friend for inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Larry's typical deflection technique, in character but predictable

jokes.text: What is that? What the fuck? What are you doing? It's a bug, a bug. I had a bug on me.
jokes.setup: Larry swatting at something during conversation
jokes.punchline: Awkward physical interruption of tense moment
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy break in tense conversation, decent timing

jokes.text: I need a black man to get my car. Is there a black man in the area who wants to take my valet ticket?
jokes.setup: Larry trying to prove he's not racist
jokes.punchline: Making it infinitely worse with over-correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry escalation - trying to fix racism with more racism

jokes.text: What's the race on this doll? It's biracial. Biracial? Really?
jokes.punchline: Treating doll's race as product specification
jokes.explanation: Larry's clinical approach to social issues creates humor

jokes.text: The toy store clerk's explanation of the biracial doll and Larry's follow-up questions about Asian-white dolls
jokes.punchline: Larry's overly analytical approach to doll demographics
jokes.explanation: Larry turning simple toy purchase into sociological inquiry

jokes.text: If somebody's Asian, and they marry a white person, are they making sort of a half-Asian, half-white doll too?
jokes.punchline: Taking concept to logical extreme with other races
jokes.explanation: Larry's systematic thinking applied inappropriately to social issues

jokes.text: It's a mulatto. No good? Biracial is what we call it, usually.
jokes.setup: Larry presenting gift at baby shower
jokes.punchline: Using outdated racial term in front of group
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social faux pas, good setup and payoff

jokes.text: That's actually for the surrogate.
jokes.punchline: Awkward reminder of surrogate situation
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier surrogate gift debate

jokes.text: Larry's extended metaphor about scripts as babies that writers lose to other writers
jokes.setup: Larry talking to the surrogate about his writing career
jokes.punchline: Larry unknowingly describing surrogate motherhood while talking to actual surrogate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic scene where Larry inadvertently influences the surrogate through oblivious metaphor

jokes.text: You never see the baby again, and it's your baby. And they give it to another writer. And that writer doesn't let you even look at your baby.
jokes.punchline: Escalating the inappropriate metaphor
jokes.explanation: Escalates the cringe perfectly, Larry completely oblivious to context

jokes.text: After three shuffle-ball chains, he's winded.
jokes.punchline: Mel Brooks pointing out Larry's poor fitness
jokes.explanation: Good comedy from guest star, economical insult

jokes.text: Are those genitals? What is that on that bull?
jokes.punchline: Elderly man shocked by anatomical accuracy
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag, relies on prudishness for humor

jokes.text: Oh, hi, Mr. Schwimmer. Uh, this is Larry David. I just want to say... you fucking asshole! What's your fucking problem, you prick?
jokes.punchline: Road rage interrupts, creates angry voicemail
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke, perfect timing for explosive payoff

jokes.text: Oh, my heart! What are you doing? What is this?
jokes.punchline: Larry faking heart attack with monitoring device
jokes.explanation: Good escalation using setup device, Larry's self-preservation instincts

jokes.text: Larry faking a heart attack to escape road rage confrontation
jokes.setup: Aggressive driver threatens Larry with tire iron
jokes.punchline: Larry removes heart monitor wires to fake medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of setup from earlier in episode, perfect Larry self-preservation

jokes.text: Seriously, the guy was... the guy was coming after me with a tire iron. I faked the whole thing.
jokes.punchline: Admitting he faked heart attack
jokes.explanation: Larry's matter-of-fact confession to outrageous behavior

jokes.text: I've dated both... black men, white men and there really isn't a difference. It's... it's really just kind of a myth. That's a myth? Boy, too bad for the black guys.
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate response to answer
jokes.explanation: Good callback with typical Larry inappropriate response

jokes.text: I bought a mulatto doll today. Okay, um, we don't really use mulatto anymore...
jokes.punchline: Another correction about racial terminology
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier racial terminology mistake

jokes.text: If we all keep fucking each other, then we're gonna be the same race sooner or later anyways. Let's pray for that.
jokes.punchline: Larry's earnest agreement with crude solution
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about social issues, Larry's earnestness makes it funnier

jokes.text: So you're taking up a hospital bed because you're afraid of some weird guy in traffic?
jokes.punchline: Cheryl pointing out absurdity of situation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman response highlighting Larry's selfishness

jokes.text: She's gone into labor. She's here at the hospital, and she's not gonna give the baby up... She said she had a talk with you.
jokes.setup: Cheryl delivering bad news about surrogate
jokes.punchline: Larry's script metaphor convinced surrogate to keep baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to setup - Larry's oblivious metaphor has real consequences

jokes.text: Those bastards. The fourth time I've been here, and I still can't get this colostomy bag to fit.
jokes.punchline: More medical issues beyond dog bite
jokes.explanation: Weak callback, piling on medical issues without strong punchline

jokes.text: You can't keep that baby! I was talking about a script. I want that panda back!
jokes.setup: Larry trying to fix surrogate situation
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to clarify metaphor, plus random panda reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's panic, random panda adds absurdity

jokes.text: What the fuck?! Why are you looking at my dick?! You looking at me?
jokes.setup: Larry looking at basketball player in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Getting caught checking size
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup about checking player sizes

jokes.text: Think I'm gonna steal your car? No no, it's just I forgot to... I forgot to just put the alarm on.
jokes.setup: Larry setting car alarm near black man
jokes.punchline: Another racial profiling incident
jokes.explanation: Callback to racial profiling theme but less impactful than first instance

jokes.text: So what, the black man in a suit parks cars. Black man, no suit, he gonna steal your car.
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the no-win situation for black men
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of racial profiling theme, shows damned-if-you-do logic

jokes.text: No black man don't want a toy car. Black man want a car with some get up and go.
jokes.punchline: Insulting Larry's car while making racial point
jokes.explanation: Good insult comedy, adding injury to Larry's embarrassment

jokes.text: Well, did you tell him I was black? No. Why didn't you tell him I was black? Larry, you don't know when to play the card.
jokes.punchline: Wanda explaining strategic use of race card
jokes.explanation: Smart commentary on liberal guilt and strategic racial awareness

jokes.text: Tell him I'm like a brown-skinned black, not light skinned. Make sure he know I'm real black.
jokes.punchline: Getting very specific about blackness for maximum effect
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation showing calculating approach to racial identity

jokes.text: Can I say that this is fantastic, the magazines? What an improvement, unbelievable... It was the best waiting room experience I've ever had.
jokes.punchline: Praising magazine improvement he complained about
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing his complaints were actually heard

jokes.text: This is Irv Schwimmer. You motherfucking, cocksucking, son-of-a-bitch bastard! Who the fuck do you think you are?!
jokes.punchline: Furious response to Larry's accidental angry message
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing escalation of misunderstanding, elderly man swearing is funny
